key_insight: The paper learns the token unmasking order in diffusion language models instead of choosing it heuristically, turning decoding order into an optimizable policy that improves reasoning quality.
engineering_relevance: For practitioners building diffusion-based or semi-autoregressive generators, learned scheduling offers a lightweight way to boost accuracy without changing the frozen denoiser architecture.
method: Derive an upper bound on sequential decoding mismatch using KL divergence and pathwise log-likelihood.
Convert order selection into a dense self-aware reward over ordered trajectories.
Train a lightweight schedule policy with Group Relative Policy Optimization and apply it to any-order and semi-autoregressive decoding.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports gains on Sudoku with 1B MDM from 82.0% to 91.8% and 97.5% after second-stage fine-tuning, plus improvements on GSM8K (64% to 76%) and MBPP (39.5% to 41%) with LLaDA-8B.
watch_item: The method depends on the quality of the learned schedule policy and may require extra optimization/fine-tuning, so gains may vary across models, decoding lengths, and block sizes.
paper_title: Scheduling Thoughts: Learning the Order of Thought in Diffusion Language Models
